excluded_as_non_requirement.reason: empty placeholder template, contains no actionable requirement text

methodology.static_trace: Every source file read in full; every CLI command traced from clap definition (cli.rs) through lib.rs::handle() dispatch to its commands.rs implementation and store.rs persistence calls.
methodology.cross_reference_check: grep-verified every `module::function()` call and every `config.field` access across all source files against their actual definitions. Zero dangling references found (no typos/missing functions that would fail to compile, no Config struct field mismatches).
methodology.compile_result: blocked - sandbox network allowlist does not include static.rust-lang.org, so `rustup toolchain install stable` could not fetch a toolchain. apt-get only provides rustc/cargo 1.75.0, but Cargo.toml declares `edition = "2024"` which requires Rust 1.85+ (also stated in the project's own README). Did not force-build with the mismatched 1.75 compiler to avoid reporting spurious edition/syntax errors that reflect the sandbox, not the code.
methodology.confidence: High for structural/referential correctness and for feature-presence/absence claims (each is backed by a specific file+function citation). Not verified: type-checker/borrow-checker-level correctness, actual runtime behavior, concurrency correctness under real parallel load.

verdict.summary: The core coordination protocol (domains, claims, messaging, park/pump, attach-and-inject-AGENTS.md) is faithfully implemented and closely mirrors the design theorized in baseline.md and brainstorm_00.md, including the deadlock-breaking cycle-detection algorithm. Five distinct gaps were found: two explicit unbuilt features (terminal/GUI relaunch, test-harness automation), two partially-built features (domain reassignment, domain visibility), and one feature that is implemented but operationally disconnected from the rest of the system (model/agent selection).

requirements.requirement: config.json should be able to set which agents are used by default
evidence: src/models.rs:AgentConfig.default
src/commands.rs:selected_agents()

requirements.requirement: --agents / --no-agents flags to specify which models/agents are enabled per session, e.g. `trelane --agents "claude-2, gpt-4, gpt-4-32k" --no-agents "gpt-3.5-turbo, gpt-3.5-turbo-16k" .`
evidence: src/cli.rs:Cli.agents / Cli.no_agents (global args)
src/commands.rs:selected_agents(), cmd_attach_project()
requirements.note: syntax matches the spec's example verbatim; see gap 'model-selection-disconnected' for a caveat on what this flag actually affects downstream

requirements.requirement: launch a trelane session on an existing project folder; agents can read and write files there (like safe_pocket)
evidence: src/commands.rs:cmd_init(), cmd_attach_project()
src/domain.rs: per-agent writable globs enforced via CompiledDomain

requirements.requirement: hook Trelane into already-running agents/sessions rather than being an entry point on its own, e.g. via injecting into local AGENTS.md — described in the source note as "probably the most important feature to add to Trelane"
evidence: src/commands.rs:inject_agents_md() writes an idempotent block between '<!-- BEGIN TRELANE -->' / '<!-- END TRELANE -->' markers
README.md:'Attach Mode' section
requirements.priority_note: source material calls this the most important feature; it is solidly built

requirements.requirement: terminal relaunch: identify open terminal tabs/windows by title and send input (incl. hitting Return) to relaunch GUI-embedded agents; research needed
evidence: docs/terminal-relaunch.md is research notes only (AppleScript/CLI snippets for Terminal.app, iTerm2, kitty, WezTerm, tmux) with no corresponding adapter code
src/cli.rs has no relaunch/adapter subcommand

requirements.requirement: account for various terminal emulators possibly having unique command inputs
evidence: docs/terminal-relaunch.md catalogs Terminal.app/iTerm2/kitty/WezTerm/tmux quirks in prose, but none of it is codified

requirements.requirement: define what a 'Trelane session' is, including agents added mid-session that need to get up to speed and be detected by others
evidence: src/db.rs: agents + session_agents tables
src/pump.rs:wake_candidates() iterates store::list_agents() fresh every tick, so newly add-agent'd agents are auto-detected on the next pump tick
src/prompt.rs:compose_prompt() gives a newly-woken agent its full current domain/inbox/parked state

requirements.requirement: use Rust, not Python, at /Users/jadennation/DEV/01_active_projects/trelane
evidence: Cargo.toml: name="trelane", edition 2024
full src/*.rs implementation

requirements.requirement: Domains: area of responsibility per agent (files/tasks/research area)
evidence: src/models.rs:Domain{writable, forbidden_write}
src/domain.rs:CompiledDomain::is_writable() (globset-based, forbidden overrides writable)

requirements.requirement: Quest: agent working outside its domain needs permission from the domain owner
evidence: src/commands.rs:cmd_claim() denies cross-domain writes unless a claim-grant message id is presented via --grant
src/commands.rs:grant_covers()

requirements.requirement: Action / no internal cron — agent checks state between actions, not on a timer
evidence: the external `trelane pump` process fills exactly this role, as theorized

requirements.requirement: Shutdown is normal, not failure; agent cannot relaunch itself
evidence: src/commands.rs:cmd_done()
README.md:'Core inversion' / 'three invariants' section
src/prompt.rs:BOOTSTRAP_TEMPLATE exit checklist

requirements.requirement: avoid deadlock via parking instead of waiting; a dumb pump does cycle detection and breaks ties (matches the 'three rules' in brainstorm_00.md nearly verbatim)
evidence: src/commands.rs:cmd_park()/cmd_unpark()
src/pump.rs:wait_graph(), dfs_cycle(), wake_candidates() (breaks ties via cycle.iter().min() = lexicographically-first agent, matching README's stated algorithm)

requirements.requirement: Launch/Relaunch via a cron-like watcher checking agents' message queues/ledger
evidence: src/pump.rs:tick() — `trelane pump --once` (cron-friendly) or `--watch` (looping)

requirements.requirement: for GUI-embedded agents, type directly into the terminal (incl. hitting Return) to relaunch them; needs research
evidence: same as api-05

requirements.requirement: messaging between agents with type/urgency/body fields
evidence: src/models.rs:Message, MSG_TYPES (question/answer/info/claim-request/claim-grant/claim-deny/handoff/system), URGENCIES (low/normal/high/critical)
src/crypto.rs: HMAC-SHA256 signing over canonical JSON
requirements.note: claim-deny and handoff types are defined and accepted by cmd_send but never produced by the stub agent or exercised by demo-rust.sh — only untested via the automated demo, not necessarily broken

requirements.requirement: domains are not permanent and can be shifted or replaced entirely during or between agent runs; shifting requires messaging between agents
evidence: src/cli.rs has no update-domain/redomain/set-domain command
src/commands.rs:cmd_add_agent() hard-errors 'agent already exists' if called twice
src/store.rs:insert_agent() is a plain INSERT, not an upsert

requirements.requirement: agents should be able to review existing domain boundaries in the current session
evidence: src/prompt.rs:compose_prompt() gives an agent its OWN domain via [[DOMAIN_JSON]]
src/commands.rs:cmd_status() lists agents/inbox/running-state but does NOT print any agent's writable/forbidden_write globs
other agents' domains are only discoverable indirectly, one path at a time, via a claim denial message

requirements.requirement: complex, thorough test harness: a somewhat complicated project testing two agents operating in individual domains with different message types passed between them
evidence: demo-rust.sh sets up 2 agents (alpha/beta) with disjoint single-glob domains and one file each; exercises question/answer and claim-request/claim-grant flows
claim-deny, handoff, and system message types are not exercised by the demo

requirements.requirement: include dangerous deadlock-causing conditions to test that the relaunch script detects and resolves deadlocks
evidence: demo-rust.sh scenario C: manufactures a total deadlock (alpha waits on beta, beta waits on alpha), then shows the pump detect the cycle, wake the designated breaker, and resolve it

requirements.requirement: run the test harness multiple times and generate a report of results per run, to see whether changes improve or worsen agent cooperation over time
evidence: demo-rust.sh is a single deterministic pass (`set -euo pipefail`); no repetition flag, no structured report output, no persisted run history

requirements.requirement: select low-cost AI models for testing so the harness can run repeatedly without high cost
evidence: demo-rust.sh uses `trelane stub`, a zero-token scripted fake agent, instead of any real (even cheap) AI model; no code path exercises real LLM behavior against the protocol

requirements.requirement: provide agents with a set of tasks to complete and measure their performance completing those tasks
evidence: src/commands.rs:cmd_stub() only auto-answers questions and auto-grants claims formulaically; it never performs or scores real work product
no task-assignment or scoring data model exists anywhere in src/

requirements.source: inferred from FEATURES/dailies/2026_07_03_api.md example syntax + code tracing (not a literal quote)
requirements.requirement: selected agents/models (--agents/--no-agents/config.json defaults) should actually govern which model an agent identity runs as and/or gate what the pump launches
evidence: src/db.rs: `session_agents` table is entirely separate from the `agents` table used by add-agent/claim/send/park/wake/pump

src/commands.rs: `session_agents` is written only by cmd_attach_project (upsert_session_agent) and read only by cmd_status (display) — grep-confirmed zero references to session_agent(s) in pump.rs, and inject_agents_md() receives the enabled/disabled lists as plain function args rather than reading them back from the table
no code anywhere maps a domain-owning agent identity (e.g. 'frontend') to a specific enabled model

gaps.title: GUI/terminal relaunch is unimplemented (research-only)
gaps.description: Headless relaunch works (trelane pump spawns agents as subprocesses via the launcher template). Relaunching an agent that is running inside an already-open GUI terminal/TUI session does not: there is no code that identifies a terminal tab/window/pane and injects text + Enter into it.
evidence: docs/terminal-relaunch.md covers Terminal.app (osascript), iTerm2 ('write text'), kitty ('kitty @ send-text'), WezTerm ('wezterm cli send-text'), and tmux ('send-keys'), each with example commands and limitations, and recommends implementation order tmux -> iTerm2 -> WezTerm -> kitty -> Terminal.app

the document's own closing line: 'For now, Trelane implements attachment by writing durable project instructions into AGENTS.md and recording enabled/disabled session agents. GUI relaunch should be added as adapter work after target identity storage is designed.'
src/cli.rs defines no relaunch/adapter subcommand
gaps.recommendation: Build the tmux adapter first per the doc's own recommendation (simplest, cross-terminal, CI-testable): a `trelane relaunch <agent> --adapter tmux --target <session:window>` command that stores the target identifier at attach/add-agent time and is invoked from pump.rs as an alternative launch path alongside the existing sh -c launcher-template path.

gaps.title: No way to change an agent's domain after creation
gaps.description: baseline.md explicitly frames domains as impermanent and shiftable during a session, but the CLI only supports creating a domain once. There is no update path.
evidence: src/cli.rs: Command enum has AddAgent but no UpdateDomain/Redomain/SetDomain variant
src/commands.rs:cmd_add_agent() returns TrelaneError::msg("agent '{name}' already exists") if the agent is already registered
src/store.rs:insert_agent() executes a plain `INSERT INTO agents` (no ON CONFLICT/REPLACE), so even bypassing the CLI check would hit a primary-key violation
gaps.recommendation: Add a `trelane redomain AGENT --writable GLOB [--desc TEXT]` command backed by an UPDATE (or DELETE+INSERT) in store.rs, and have it emit an `info`-type broadcast message to affected/overlapping agents per baseline.md's 'shifting domains requires messaging between all agents'.

gaps.title: No command surfaces all agents' domains together
gaps.description: An agent can see its own writable/forbidden globs (substituted into its bootstrap prompt), but there is no way to see the whole swarm's domain map at once; other agents' domains are only inferable one file at a time via a claim-denial error message.
evidence: src/commands.rs:cmd_status() prints agent name / running state / inbox count / session agents / parked tasks / claims / deadlock cycle, but never each agent's writable/forbidden_write globs
gaps.recommendation: Cheap fix: have cmd_status() also print each agent's writable globs (store::get_domain() is already available and used elsewhere).

gaps.title: Test harness covers protocol mechanics but not repeatability, real models, or task performance
gaps.description: demo-rust.sh is a genuine, working single-shot smoke test (including a real manufactured-and-resolved deadlock), but more.md asked for a repeatable, report-generating harness that runs real (low-cost) AI models against measurable tasks — none of that exists.
evidence: demo-rust.sh has no loop/repeat flag, no report file output, and no persisted run-to-run history
demo-rust.sh uses `trelane stub` (src/commands.rs:cmd_stub) — a zero-token scripted agent — never a real model
no data model anywhere for assigning agents concrete tasks or scoring completion quality
gaps.additional_note: src/pump.rs's cycle-detection algorithm (wait_graph/dfs_cycle) — arguably the single most safety-critical piece of the protocol — has zero #[test] coverage, and neither does src/store.rs. Existing unit tests (13 total) are in src/crypto.rs (6), src/domain.rs (5), src/commands.rs (2) only. All confidence in the deadlock-breaking guarantee currently rests on the one manual demo-rust.sh run.
gaps.recommendation: Short term: add #[test] cases for wait_graph/dfs_cycle (multi-node cycles, no-cycle case, cycle-among-non-running-agents-only). Longer term: wrap demo-rust.sh with a --repeat N and a JSON/CSV results emitter, and add an opt-in scenario that swaps the stub launcher for a real low-cost-model launcher template against a small fixed task list with pass/fail criteria.

gaps.title: --agents/--no-agents/config.json default-agents selection is display-only, not operational
gaps.description: The CLI syntax and config schema for selecting which models/agents are enabled per session match FEATURES/dailies/2026_07_03_api.md's example exactly, but tracing the data flow shows the selection is never consulted by anything that actually launches or gates an agent.
evidence: src/db.rs: `session_agents` table is structurally separate from the `agents` table (the one add-agent/claim/send/park/wake/pump operate on) with no foreign key or join between them

grep across src/*.rs: session_agents is written only in src/commands.rs:cmd_attach_project() (via store::upsert_session_agent) and read only in src/commands.rs:cmd_status() (for display) and indirectly copied into the AGENTS.md text by inject_agents_md()
src/pump.rs contains zero references to session_agents, selected_agents, or AgentConfig — the wake/launch path is entirely unaware of which models were enabled/disabled
gaps.recommendation: Decide the intended scope: either (a) wire session_agents into cmd_add_agent validation and/or the launcher-template substitution so 'disabled' models genuinely cannot be woken, or (b) if it's meant to stay purely informational (a note to whichever human/AI reads AGENTS.md), document that explicitly in README.md's 'Attach Mode' section so it isn't mistaken for enforcement.

minor_issues.title: prompts/bootstrap.md at the project root is a stale duplicate
minor_issues.description: The checked-in prompts/bootstrap.md still instructs agents to run `python3 swarmctl.py <command>` and references a `.swarm/` directory — leftovers from before this became the Rust `trelane` CLI. The actual runtime template is the BOOTSTRAP_TEMPLATE constant in src/prompt.rs, which correctly uses `trelane <command>` / `.trelane/`, and is what cmd_init() writes to .trelane/prompts/bootstrap.md and what compose_prompt() actually sends to agents.
minor_issues.impact: no functional bug (nothing in src/ reads the root-level file), but it will mislead a future contributor who opens it expecting it to be current
evidence: prompts/bootstrap.md line ~9: 'python3 swarmctl.py <command> ...'
src/prompt.rs:BOOTSTRAP_TEMPLATE line ~10: 'trelane <command> ...'
minor_issues.recommendation: Delete prompts/bootstrap.md from the repo root, or replace it with a comment pointing to src/prompt.rs as the source of truth, or have cmd_init() write from a shared template file instead of duplicating the string as a Rust constant.

minor_issues.title: .env's SPOCKET_ROOT is unreferenced
minor_issues.description: `.env` sets SPOCKET_ROOT=/Users/jadennation/.safe_pocket/c92b2eb092e7 but no src/*.rs file reads it (grep-confirmed).
minor_issues.impact: cosmetic; likely a leftover dev convenience variable
evidence: .env: SPOCKET_ROOT=...

minor_issues.title: store::get_all_messages_for_agent is defined but never called
minor_issues.description: Looks like scaffolding for a future 'message history' command (as opposed to get_unprocessed_messages, which is used everywhere) that doesn't exist yet in cli.rs.
minor_issues.impact: none functionally; flagged for awareness only
evidence: src/store.rs:get_all_messages_for_agent()

recommended_priority.reason: small surface area; baseline.md treats domain shifting as core to the model, not optional
cheap to resolve either direction (wire it up or document it as informational), but leaving it ambiguous risks someone relying on 'disabled' models actually being blocked when they aren't
cheap win — store::get_domain() already exists and is used elsewhere; just needs to be added to cmd_status() output
largest remaining greenfield feature; doc's own recommended order (tmux first) is a reasonable starting scope
largest remaining greenfield feature; start with unit tests for pump.rs::wait_graph/dfs_cycle before building out the full repeat+report+real-model harness
